category_title: Preschoolers with aggressive behaviors and social-emotional regulation difficulties
minimal_context: The student is asked to clean up toys and transition to the reading rug with the rest of the preschool class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to clean up the toys and transition to the reading rug.

STUDENT: No, I am not going.

TUTOR: We need to follow the classroom procedures and transition now.

STUDENT: I am not following the rules!

TUTOR: You need to come sit down with the rest of the class.

STUDENT: No! Do what I say or I will hurt you!

options.A: Give the student a direct verbal warning regarding his defiant behavior and refusal to transition to the reading rug.
options.B: Use a transition song ('It is time to clean up') beginning 10 minutes before the transition, repeating it three times at 3-minute intervals.
options.C: Use a 'request for clarification,' asking the student for explanations and elaborations regarding his refusal to transition to the reading rug.
options.D: Give the student a hug and tell him he is a lovely person who will soon find friends on the reading rug.
